summary: Typosquat of cssnano postcss-minify-selectors with side-effect load of typosquat parser dependency
details: Package name 'postcss-minify-selector' (singular) is a one-character omission typosquat of the widely-used cssnano plugin 'postcss-minify-selectors' (plural). The package reinforces the impersonation by registering its PostCSS plugin under the legitimate name: src/index.js line 213 sets `postcssPlugin: 'postcss-minify-selectors'`, so consumers inspecting the loaded plugin see the real plugin's identity. On require, src/index.js line 2 unconditionally executes `require('postcss-minify-selector-parser/cjs-runner')` — a side-effect import of a `/cjs-runner` subpath of a separately typosquat-named dependency (the legitimate parser used by the real plugin is 'postcss-selector-parser'). Pulling and loading that typosquat-named transitive at require time runs whatever code its `cjs-runner` entry contains in the installer's process, with no caller opt-in. The combination of name impersonation, identity spoofing in the plugin metadata, and an unconditional load of a typosquat-named sibling package's runner subpath is the typosquat-loader shape used to drop unverified code into projects that mistype the cssnano plugin name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
